clauses.statement: Every newly recorded judged_unsound verdict declares exactly one supported cause, implementation_unsound or classifier_premise_unsound; a cause on another verdict is rejected, while legacy cause-less unsound verdicts normalize to implementation_unsound.

Judgment adjudication writes schema 0.2.0 as an append-only event log with stable event ids and explicit correction references; recording a root verdict never removes or replaces an earlier event, and migrated v1 events retain an explicit legacy_origin marker.

A premise correction is accepted only for a current-HEAD classifier_premise_unsound verdict in the same story and item, and only when it supplies nonblank wrong/corrected premises, an auditable reason, closed-set provenance, and at least one readable workspace-relative regular file whose SHA-256 is recorded; symlinks and realpath escapes are rejected before persistence.

Current judgment state is resolved from explicit same-item, same-HEAD event references rather than array order; duplicate, dangling, cross-item, cross-HEAD, branched, cyclic, unknown-cause, invalid-evidence, and schema/model-payload mismatch histories fail closed instead of downgrading v2 to legacy.

A classifier premise correction leaves the gate in needs_evidence until a different independent current-HEAD judge records a verdict linked to that correction; linked judged_sound resolves the item automatically, linked needs_human_judgment uses the existing accepted decision path, and implementation_unsound or linked judged_unsound remains failed.

The CLI and generated judgment request expose unsound cause classification, adjudicate correct, correction-linked re-adjudication, and the prohibition on using premise correction as a generic waiver; English/Japanese README and gate-evidence skill document the same lifecycle.

diagrams.title: Classifier premise recovery state transitions
diagrams.mermaid:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> failed: judged_unsound
  failed --> needs_evidence: classifier premise correction
  needs_evidence --> passed: linked judged_sound
  needs_evidence --> passed: linked needs-human + accepted decision
  needs_evidence --> failed: linked judged_unsound
  failed --> failed: implementation_unsound

diagrams.title: Append-only correction lineage
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  V["Original unsound verdict"] --> C["Premise correction + evidence hash"]
  C --> R["Fresh linked re-adjudication"]
  R --> S{"Resolver"}
  S -->|sound| P["passed"]
  S -->|unsound| F["failed"]

diagrams.title: Adjudication lineage trust boundaries
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Judge["Independent judge"] --> CLI["VibePro adjudicate CLI"]
  CLI --> Validate["Story / item / HEAD / provenance validation"]
  Evidence["Workspace-relative evidence file"] --> Hash["Readable regular file + SHA-256"]
  Hash --> Validate
  Tamper["Forged or stale lineage"] --> Validate
  Validate -->|reject| Closed["Fail closed"]
  Validate -->|accept| Log["Append-only adjudication log"]
  Log --> Gate["Current-state resolver and Gate"]
